title: Dactylorhiza hoeppneri
intro: Dactylorhiza hoeppneri, also known as Hoeppner's orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Europe, characterized by its unique, upright spikes of purple flowers. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza hoeppneri include its potential to lower blood pressure, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Dactylorhiza hoeppneri is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its use is often limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza hoeppneri is a species of orchid, classified within the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Dactylorhiza, which comprises approximately 40 species of terrestrial orchids commonly known as marsh orchids. The genus Dactylorhiza is further divided into several sections, with Dactylorhiza hoeppneri typically classified within the section Dactylorhiza subsect. biporitaneae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its flower shape and leaf structure. By understanding the classification of Dactylorhiza hoeppneri, botanists can better understand the plant's evolutionary history and its relationships to other species within the genus.
